title: Flickingeria quadriloba
intro: Flickingeria quadriloba is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique quadrilobed leaves and fragrant flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Flickingeria quadriloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Flickingeria quadriloba is commonly prepared as a tea or a decoction, or used in combination with other herbs to create topical applications and tonics for various health concerns.
taxonomy: Flickingeria quadriloba is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. At the genus level, it is a member of the genus Flickingeria. Further classification places it within the subgenus Flickingeria. The species Flickingeria quadriloba is native to Southeast Asia.
